items.title: Haaland grabs hat-trick as Man City thrash Liverpool to reach FA Cup semis
items.summary: Erling Haaland treble and goal by Antoine Semenyo give City 4-0 win, as Liverpool's Mohamed Salah misses a penalty.

items.title: Israeli strikes damage hospital in Lebanon’s Tyre; ground invasion advances
items.summary: At least 11 people are injured as Israel strikes near the Lebanese Italian Hospital in Tyre, damaging the facility.

items.title: Projectile hits near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one: IAEA
items.summary: Tehran says it is the fourth attack near the nuclear plant amid the US-Israel war on Iran.

items.title: Israel’s unchecked war machine
items.summary: With conflicts on multiple fronts, Israel’s war fever shows no sign of easing.

items.title: At least four people killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine
items.summary: At least one person has also died in a Ukrainian attack on Russia's southern Rostov region.

items.title: Manchester City vs Liverpool 4-0: FA Cup quarterfinal – as it happened
items.summary: All the updates as Man City book an FA Cup semifinal spot after dismantling Liverpool at the Etihad Stadium.

items.title: Iran executes two convicted members of banned opposition group
items.summary: Abolhassan Montazer and Vahid Baniamerian have been hanged after Iran's Supreme Court upheld their sentences.

items.title: Afghanistan earthquake kills eight members of same family
items.summary: The household's only survivor was a young boy who was injured in the tremor.

items.title: NASA unveils first images of Earth from Artemis II
items.summary: NASA has unveiled the first images of Earth taken by Artemis II astronauts as they head towards the moon.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 36 of US-Israeli attacks?
items.summary: Iran claims responsibility for downing two US warplanes, as search under way for missing crewmember.

items.title: European Parliament’s Rima Hassan: I’m a target of ‘political harassment’
items.summary: European Parliament member Rima Hassan says she’s facing “genuine judicial and political harassment”.

items.title: Iranian missile attack hits residential areas in central Israel
items.summary: An Iranian missile attack has caused damage across residential areas in central Israel, according to Israeli media.

items.title: Palestinian Christians in Gaza mark Good Friday
items.summary: Palestinian Christians in Gaza marked Good Friday at the Holy Family Church during a fragile ceasefire.

items.title: Atletico Madrid vs Barcelona: La Liga preview – teams, start time, lineups
items.summary: Barcelona will be without Raphinha for all three rapid-fire matches against Atletico Madrid in the coming weeks.

items.title: Iran war live: US-Israel attacks hit petrochemical, nuclear sites in Iran
items.summary: US and Israel step up attacks on Iran civilian infrastructure as search for downed US pilot continues.

items.title: Video shows the moment a Russian drone hit an apartment in Ukraine
items.summary: Russia has unleashed a massive round of deadly air strikes across Ukraine, in a so-called "Easter escalation."

items.title: Lawyers search for Epstein survivors for Bank of America $72.5m settlement
items.summary: Bank of America has become the third major bank to settle with victims over allegations of facilitating Epstein crimes.

items.title: Christians in Lebanon observe Good Friday under Israeli attacks
items.summary: Christians across Lebanon observed Good Friday, praying for peace as Israeli strikes and evacuation orders continue.

items.title: Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: Some countries threaten action against Iran's blockade of waterway.

items.title: Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure
items.summary: There have been emotional scenes in Cuba where 2,000 prisoners are being pardoned and released from jail.

items.title: Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
items.summary: Survivors of an Israeli-US attack on Iran’s biggest road bridge have spoken of their terror as missiles hit it.

items.title: NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission
items.summary: The crew were about 100,000 miles (160,000 kilometres) from Earth and were quickly closing in on the moon.

items.title: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
items.summary: Rights groups have raised concerns about Trump's efforts to change election administration before midterms.

items.title: US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
items.summary: Judge Boasberg says Trump administration's arguments 'do not come close to convincing Court' of subpoenas' legitimacy.

items.title: More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
items.summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says 'multiple attacks' on Iranian healthcare facilities have been reported.
